html:
Ancient Statues are structures found in the Ruins and part of the remnants of the Ancient Civilization.

When mined, they will drop a Gem and 1 Thulecite, plus a 5% chance of 1 extra Thulecite. The color of gem that drops will be the same type that appears on the statue, either in the staff or at the base. If the statue does not have a gem embedded in it, it will drop Nightmare Fuel in its place.

In Don't Starve, the color changes when reloading the game or travelling to the cave level and back. This makes it possible to choose which gems the statues will drop when mined; gemless statues will remain gemless however.

The appearance of the statues change depending on the current phase of the Ruin's Nightmare Cycle. They emit light during all phases, except "Calm". At the most intense state, the statues will be covered in Nightmare Fuel and gain a hazy glowing quality (though their light radius does not increase).  If mined during the Nightmare phase, there is a 30% chance for a Nightmarebeak or Crawling Nightmare to spawn, with equal chances.

 Downloadable Content

In the Hamlet DLC, gem in Ancient Statue can be removed with Ball Pein Hammer.


Bugs

  • The damaged versions of these statues will revert to its original form after saving and reloading the game.

Gallery

  • Mage statue as it appears with a gem in its socket.
  • Head statue as it appears with a gem in its socket.
  • Stages of breakdown for the Mage.
  • Stages of breakdown for the Head.
  • Stages of breakdown.
  • Stages of breakdown.
  • The gems as they appear in the head statue base socket.
  • The gems as they appear in the mage statue staffs.
  • Statues glowing during the "Warning" phase.
  • Statues glowing during the "Nightmare" phase.
  • Example of the color of the gem changing with the Nightmare Cycle.
  • The appearance of the Ancient Mage Statue prior to implementation.
  • The appearance of the Ancient Head Statue prior to implementation.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“It seems to throb out of tune with the world.”
–Wilson
“Drat, won't burn.”
–Willow
“Ugly, ugly, ugly.”
–Wolfgang
“Not very attractive.”
–Wendy
“A NIGHTMARE INDICATOR”
–WX-78
“This seems mystically aligned to the world.”
–Wickerbottom
“Another hoser.”
–Woodie
“A once proud race.”
–Maxwell
“An outstanding resource for dark fuel conduits.”
–Wagstaff
“Treasure with mysteriöus aura.”
–Wigfrid
“It's throbbing strangely.”
–Webber
“How depressing.”
–Walani
“It gives off strange vibrations.”
–Warly
“Me ain't be likin'th'vibes it be givin'.”
–Woodlegs
“IT GIVETH WILBA HEEBIE-GEEBIES”
–Wilba
“Alive? Hmmm...”
–Wormwood
“It looks... old.”
–Wheeler
“I'd never wanna meet one in person.”
–Winona
“Hyuyu, how spooky!”
–Wortox
“Creepy.”
–Wurt
“Woah! Is this what they looked like?”
–Walter
“What's left from a time long ago.”
–Wanda
Ancient Statues are structures found in the Ruins and part of the remnants of the Ancient Civilization.
When mined, they will drop a Gem and 1 Thulecite, plus a 5% chance of 1 extra Thulecite. The color of gem that drops will be the same type that appears on the statue, either in the staff or at the base. If the statue does not have a gem embedded in it, it will drop Nightmare Fuel in its place.
In Don't Starve, the color changes when reloading the game or travelling to the cave level and back. This makes it possible to choose which gems the statues will drop when mined; gemless statues will remain gemless however.
The appearance of the statues change depending on the current phase of the Ruin's Nightmare Cycle. They emit light during all phases, except "Calm". At the most intense state, the statues will be covered in Nightmare Fuel and gain a hazy glowing quality (though their light radius does not increase).  If mined during the Nightmare phase, there is a 30% chance for a Nightmarebeak or Crawling Nightmare to spawn, with equal chances.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
In the Hamlet DLC, gem in Ancient Statue can be removed with Ball Pein Hammer.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
The damaged versions of these statues will revert to its original form after saving and reloading the game.

Is this what they looked like? |wanda = What's left from a time long ago. |choose = wagstaff}} '''Ancient Statues''' are structures found in the [[Ruins]] and part of the remnants of the [[Ancient Civilization]]. When mined, they will drop a [[Gem]] and 1 [[Thulecite]], plus a 5% chance of 1 extra Thulecite. The color of gem that drops will be the same type that appears on the statue, either in the staff or at the base. If the statue does not have a gem embedded in it, it will drop [[Nightmare Fuel]] in its place. In ''[[Don't Starve]]'', the color changes when reloading the game or travelling to the cave level and back. This makes it possible to choose which gems the statues will drop when mined; gemless statues will remain gemless however. The appearance of the statues change depending on the current phase of the Ruin's Nightmare Cycle. They emit light during all phases, except "Calm". At the most intense state, the statues will be covered in Nightmare Fuel and gain a hazy glowing quality (though their light radius does not increase).  If mined during the Nightmare phase, there is a 30% chance for a [[Shadow Creature#Terrorbeak|Nightmarebeak]] or [[Shadow Creature#Crawling Horror|Crawling Nightmare]] to spawn, with equal chances. =={{pic|32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC, gem in Ancient Statue can be removed with [[Ball Pein Hammer]]. __NOTOC__ == {{pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * The damaged versions of these statues will revert to its original form after saving and reloading the game. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Ancient Mage Gemmed.png|Mage statue as it appears with a gem in its socket.
